summary:
The main cause of greater fracture risk after root canal therapy in back teeth lies in their structural changes due to open canals reducing internal support; this differs from front teeth where similar crown damage doesn't significantly increase breakage likelihood as much. Key point here being altered inner tooth composition rather than just outer surface integrity issues alone driving heightened vulnerability observed clinically with these specific types of restoration failures. 

The summary captures that the critical factor involves changed interior structures affecting strength differently between molars vs other areas despite both having some degree of compromised external
